title: _zstd: ZstdCompressor.last_mode written plain under the lock but read lock-free via its Py_T_INT member descriptor
description: ZstdCompressor.flush()/.compress() store the int last_mode field with a plain C write under self->lock (compressor.c:679), but last_mode is exposed as a read-only Py_T_INT member, so getattr(c,'last_mode') reads it with a relaxed-atomic load in PyMember_GetOne (structmember.c:64) without the lock. On a shared compressor the unlocked plain write races the unlocked atomic read.
sites: Python/structmember.c PyMember_GetOne:64 (FT_ATOMIC_LOAD_INT_RELAXED of the Py_T_INT last_mode member, no lock)
Modules/_zstd/compressor.c _zstd_ZstdCompressor_flush_impl:679 (plain write self->last_mode = mode, under self->lock)
Modules/_zstd/compressor.c _zstd_ZstdCompressor_flush_impl:682 (plain write self->last_mode = ZSTD_e_end, reset path)
Modules/_zstd/compressor.c _zstd_ZstdCompressor_compress_impl:631/634 (same plain writes, same race face)
Modules/_zstd/compressor.c ZstdCompressor_members:759 (last_mode published as read-only Py_T_INT member)
race_kind: plain write vs lock-free relaxed-atomic read (mutable int field exposed as Py_T_INT member; write side not converted to atomic)
shared_state: ZstdCompressor.last_mode (int; last compression end-directive). Writes guarded by self->PyMutex lock; member-descriptor read path is lock-free.
severity: low (value-benign, crash-free; aligned 4-byte int holding one of three enum constants; genuine TSan data race on read-only-looking getattr + flush()/compress())
repro_reliability: reliable (exit 66 on every run; ~8 threads/compressor, fresh compressor per round behind a Barrier)
suggested_fix: Convert the four last_mode stores (compressor.c:631,634,679,682) to FT_ATOMIC_STORE_INT_RELAXED to pair with the relaxed-atomic FT_ATOMIC_LOAD_INT_RELAXED already used by PyMember_GetOne; no new lock needed on the read path.
notes: General 'numeric field mutated by a plain store but published as a Py_T_* member and read lock-free via PyMember_GetOne' class. _zstd is new in 3.14; the member-read relaxed atomics landed with free-threading hardening but the module-side stores were not converted to match. Worth a small upstream PR; low priority given the benign value. Audit other _zstd (and other C-module) mutable Py_T_INT members for the same pattern.
assessment: real, new (no FT zstd race filed; incomplete atomic conversion)

related_issues.note: Modules/_zstd/ is NOT on the gh-116738 audit list (newer 3.14 module); last_mode stores still plain on current main.

fleet02_notes: fleet-02 flush|flush vehicle (TSAN-0017) folded in: same last_mode race, dedup artifact
